preamble: The transformation of a voiced aspirated velar plosive /gʱ/ into a voiceless glottal fricative /h/ is a well-documented phonetic shift, typically occurring through a process known as debuccalization. This involves the loss of the original place of articulation (velar), a reduction in the manner of articulation from a plosive to a fricative, and a change in phonation from voiced to voiceless. This type of lenition is a common sound change across various language families.
